name: Liverworts and Hornworts
description: [FILL IN] Liverworts (division Marchantiophyta) and hornworts (division Anthocerotophyta) are non-vascular land plants (bryophytes) that, together with mosses, represent the earliest diverging lineages of land plants. They lack true roots, stems, and leaves, anchoring themselves with hair-like rhizoids. Liverworts are divided into thallose (flat, ribbon-like body) and leafy (with leaf-like lobes on a stem) forms. Hornworts are distinguished by their elongated, horn-shaped sporophytes. Both groups are found in moist habitats worldwide and play important roles in nutrient cycling and soil formation.
etymology: [FILL IN] 'Liverwort' derives from Old English 'lifer' (liver) and 'wyrt' (plant), from the supposed resemblance of thallose forms to a liver. 'Hornwort' refers to the horn-like sporophytes.

distribution.habitat: [FILL IN] Moist forests, stream banks, rock faces, soil, tree bark; some species in arid habitats.
distribution.altitude_range_m: [FILL IN] Sea level to alpine zones.

morphology.growth_form: [FILL IN] Non-vascular; gametophyte-dominant life cycle. Liverworts: thallose or leafy. Hornworts: thallose with horn-like sporophytes.
morphology.height_m: [FILL IN] Typically a few millimetres to a few centimetres.
morphology.stem: [FILL IN] No true stems; liverwort gametophytes may have stem-like axes bearing leaf-like lobes (leafy liverworts).
morphology.leaves: [FILL IN] No true leaves; liverworts have leaf-like lobes (phyllidia) often in two to three ranks; hornworts have flat thalli.
morphology.reproductive_structures: [FILL IN] Liverworts: archegonia (female) and antheridia (male); sporophytes small, short-lived. Hornworts: horn-shaped sporophytes that grow continuously from a basal meristem.
morphology.seeds_or_spores: [FILL IN] Reproduce by spores; asexual reproduction by gemmae (liverworts) or fragmentation.
morphology.roots: [FILL IN] Rhizoids (single-celled in liverworts; multicellular in hornworts); anchor the plant and absorb water.

ecology.seed_dispersal: [FILL IN] Spore dispersal by wind; gemmae dispersed by water splash and animals.
ecology.symbioses: [FILL IN] Hornworts host nitrogen-fixing cyanobacteria (Nostoc) in mucilage-filled cavities within the thallus. Some liverworts associate with mycorrhizal fungi.

uses.medicinal: [FILL IN] Some liverworts (e.g., Marchantia) used in traditional medicine.
uses.horticultural: [FILL IN] Used in terrariums, vivaria, and as moisture indicators.
uses.economic: [FILL IN] Some species used in floristry and as research models (Marchantia polymorpha is a key model organism).

cultivation.soil: [FILL IN] Moist, well-aerated substrate; peat, bark, or mineral soil.
cultivation.light: [FILL IN] Indirect light to deep shade.

notable_species.notes: [FILL IN] Major model organism in plant biology; thallose liverwort.
[FILL IN] Common thallose liverwort of stream banks.
[FILL IN] Widespread hornwort of disturbed arable ground.
